---
title: "Is LLM as a judge really a good Metric"
description: "What classical test theory can tell us about the quality of the most widely common LLM evaluation metric."
author: "Malte Nalenz"
date: "2026-05-20"
categories: [template, examples]
image: thumbnail.png
image-alt: "Example thumbnail for the post"
draft: false
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: false
    fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Reliability
Experiment: some content, but different personas, styles, formats for test-retest reliability.
Readup: Test-Retest, Reliabitlity in Test theory

## Validity
Experiment: Groundedness produce data for which we know the degree of groundedness, then compare with LLM as judge
e.g. 
question is provide summary about person on certain dimensions (e.g. early life, eduaction, influence).
For each have a source. 
Then gradually increase grounding to produce an answer and make up the missing parts. then have LLM judge it.

## Directional correctness

## Ordinal vs. Metric scale
